description: Write component tests for TaskList and AddTaskForm using React Testing Library, mocking the server action imports to verify UI behavior, user interactions, optimistic updates, and error handling.
steps: Create __tests__/components/TaskList.test.tsx to test the TaskList component
Mock @/app/actions module (toggleTask, updateTask, deleteTask) at the module level with vi.mock

Test TaskList rendering: (a) renders empty state message when tasks array is empty, (b) renders all tasks with their titles, (c) shows completed tasks with line-through styling, (d) renders Edit and Delete buttons for each task
Test TaskList toggle interaction: (a) clicking checkbox calls toggleTask with correct args and optimistically updates UI, (b) on toggleTask failure, UI rolls back to original state
Test TaskList delete interaction: (a) clicking Delete removes task optimistically, (b) on deleteTask failure, UI rolls back

Test TaskList edit flow: (a) clicking Edit shows input with current title, (b) Save button calls updateTask with trimmed title, (c) Cancel button reverts to view mode, (d) empty edit title does not save
Create __tests__/components/AddTaskForm.test.tsx to test the AddTaskForm component
Mock @/app/actions module (createTask) at the module level

Test AddTaskForm: (a) renders input and Add button, (b) shows client-side error when submitting empty title, (c) calls createTask on valid submission, (d) resets form on successful creation, (e) displays server error from createTask response
Run 'source /root/.nvm/nvm.sh && npx vitest run' to confirm all component tests pass
acceptance_criteria: All component tests pass with 'npx vitest run'
TaskList tests cover: empty state, rendering tasks, toggle optimistic update + rollback, delete optimistic update + rollback, edit flow (start, save, cancel)
AddTaskForm tests cover: rendering, validation error, successful submission, server error display
Tests use @testing-library/user-event for interactions where possible
Tests query by accessible roles/labels rather than implementation details
